title: Calcearia villosa
intro: Calceolaria villosa, also known as the woolly pocketbook, is a flowering plant species native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Calceolaria villosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Calceolaria villosa is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
